title: Preserve debug logging inside a refactored case-to-heads chain
prompt:
Given:

```
def process(event) do
  case event.type do
    :login ->
      Logger.debug("Processing login for #{event.user_id}")
      MyApp.Auth.handle_login(event)
    :logout ->
      Logger.debug("Processing logout for #{event.user_id}")
      MyApp.Auth.handle_logout(event)
    :signup ->
      Logger.debug("Processing signup")
      MyApp.Auth.handle_signup(event)
  end
end
```

Refactor to multi-clause function heads. Preserve the `Logger.debug/1` calls — they must still be emitted. Destructure the relevant fields in each head. Hint: you'll need to pattern-match `user_id` in the login/logout heads but not in signup. Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.EventProcessor do ... end`.
tier_rationale: Sonnet sometimes loses side effects when converting to function heads. Tests preservation.
